title: HOMER11 tiered storage can duplicate cold data when hot source delete fails with DuckLake SQLite database is locked
author: pavel-z1
body:
## Description

We are running HOMER11 with DuckLake hot storage on local disk and cold storage on RustFS/S3.

Automatic tiering is enabled.

Expected behavior:

```text
old hot partition -> copied to cold -> deleted from hot
```

Actual behavior under live ingest load:

```text
old hot partition -> copied to cold -> delete from hot fails -> partition is still reported as moved
```

Because the source partition still exists in hot storage, it remains eligible for future tiering cycles and may be copied to cold again.

This can create multiple cold copies of the same logical data and cause uncontrolled cold storage growth.

## Environment

```text
HOMER image: ghcr.io/sipcapture/homer:latest
HOMER version/branch: homer11
Storage backend: DuckLake
Hot storage: local parquet
Cold storage: RustFS/S3
Hot catalog: SQLite
Cold catalog: SQLite
```

Relevant configuration:

```text
HOMER_STORAGE_DUCKLAKE_CATALOG_TYPE=sqlite
HOMER_STORAGE_DUCKLAKE_CATALOG_PATH=/data/homer/homer_catalog.sqlite
HOMER_STORAGE_DUCKLAKE_DATA_PATH=/data/homer/parquet

HOMER_NODE_DUCKLAKE_VOLUMES_0_NAME=hot
HOMER_NODE_DUCKLAKE_VOLUMES_0_TYPE=local
HOMER_NODE_DUCKLAKE_VOLUMES_0_CATALOG_TYPE=sqlite
HOMER_NODE_DUCKLAKE_VOLUMES_0_CATALOG_PATH=/data/homer/homer_catalog.sqlite
HOMER_NODE_DUCKLAKE_VOLUMES_0_PATH=/data/homer/parquet

HOMER_NODE_DUCKLAKE_VOLUMES_1_NAME=cold
HOMER_NODE_DUCKLAKE_VOLUMES_1_TYPE=s3
HOMER_NODE_DUCKLAKE_VOLUMES_1_CATALOG_TYPE=sqlite
HOMER_NODE_DUCKLAKE_VOLUMES_1_CATALOG_PATH=/data/homer/homer_catalog_cold.sqlite
HOMER_NODE_DUCKLAKE_VOLUMES_1_PATH=s3://homer-data-cold
HOMER_NODE_DUCKLAKE_VOLUMES_1_S3_ENDPOINT=http://rustfs:9000
HOMER_NODE_DUCKLAKE_VOLUMES_1_S3_USE_SSL=false

HOMER_STORAGE_DUCKLAKE_STORAGE_POLICY_ENABLE=true
HOMER_STORAGE_DUCKLAKE_STORAGE_POLICY_CONCURRENT_MOVES=1
HOMER_STORAGE_DUCKLAKE_STORAGE_POLICY_VOLUMES_0_MAX_DATA_AGE_DAYS=2
HOMER_STORAGE_DUCKLAKE_STORAGE_POLICY_VOLUMES_0_MAX_SIZE_GB=5000
HOMER_STORAGE_DUCKLAKE_STORAGE_POLICY_VOLUMES_1_MAX_DATA_AGE_DAYS=7

HOMER_STORAGE_DUCKLAKE_COMPACTION_ENABLE=true
HOMER_STORAGE_DUCKLAKE_COMPACTION_RETENTION_DAYS=2
```

Example ingest load:

```text
Writer stats (5min) received=2140312 dropped=74 drop_queue=0 drop_err=5 drop_dup=69 processed=2140238 connections=0 ducklake_buf_rows=405762 ducklake_tables=10
```

## Observed behavior

Tiering starts normally:

```text
2026-07-13T12:31:02Z INF TieringService: Starting tiering cycle
2026-07-13T12:31:02Z INF TieringService: TTL partition scan source=hot dest=cold max_data_age_days=2 partition_date_cutoff=2026-07-11
2026-07-13T12:31:03Z INF TieredStorageManager: Moving partition table=hep_proto_1_default date=2026-07-11T00:00:00Z from=hot to=cold
```

The partition is copied to cold storage, but deleting it from hot storage fails:

```text
2026-07-13T12:34:56Z WRN TieredStorageManager: Failed to delete from source after successful insert table=hep_proto_1_default date=2026-07-11T00:00:00Z error="TransactionContext Error: Failed to commit: Failed to commit DuckLake transaction.
Failed to flush changes into DuckLake: database is locked"
```

HOMER still reports the partition as moved and completes the tiering cycle:

```text
2026-07-13T12:34:56Z INF TieredStorageManager: Partition moved table=hep_proto_1_default date=2026-07-11T00:00:00Z rows=334151232
2026-07-13T12:34:56Z INF TieringService: Tiering cycle completed duration=3m53.791210886s partitions_moved=1
```

## Why this is a problem

After the failed source delete:

```text
cold copy exists
hot source still exists
tiering cycle is reported as successful
hot partition remains eligible for the next tiering cycle
```

So the next tiering cycle can copy the same hot partition to cold again.

This means cold storage may grow even though the data was already copied before.

## Impact

During testing, RustFS cold storage grew to about `900G` while automatic tiering was enabled. The same hot data were copied to the cold storage four times.

The multipart upload directory was empty:

```text
0 /var/lib/docker/volumes/rustfs_data/_data/.rustfs.sys/multipart
```

So the growth was not caused by failed multipart uploads. It was finalized cold parquet data created by HOMER tiering.

We had to manually reset the cold catalog and RustFS bucket to recover disk space. This makes automatic hot-to-cold tiering unsafe under this workload.

## Expected behavior

If the hot source delete fails after a successful cold copy, HOMER should not treat the partition move as fully successful.

Expected behavior should be one of:

1. Retry the hot source delete.
2. Return an error from the move operation.
3. Do not log `Partition moved` if source delete failed.
4. Mark the partition as partially moved and avoid copying it again.
5. On the next cycle, detect the existing cold copy and do delete-only from hot.
6. Use the same catalog locking/retry mechanism as writer flush and compaction.

title: PCAP Uploader sends `Authorization: undefined` instead of bearer token
author: yishayn
body: ## Bug description The **PCAP Uploader** widget fails when attempting to upload a previously saved PCAP file. The user interface displays the following error: ```text Invalid authorization header ``` example screenshot to illustrate: Browser Developer Tools show that the upload request is sent with an invalid `Authorization` header: ```http POST /api/v4/imports/pcap HTTP/1.1 Authorization: undefined ``` The PCAP Uploader therefore appears to submit the literal JavaScript value `undefined` instead of the authenticated user's bearer token. ## Environment - HOMER version: `11.0.289` - Container image: `ghcr.io/sipcapture/homer:11.0.289` - Deployment method: Docker Compose - Host OS: `Rocky Linux 9.7` - Docker Engine version: `29.2.1` - Docker Compose version: `v5.1.0` - Browser: `Google Chrome, Version 150.0.7871.101 (Official Build) (64-bit)` - Authentication method: `[local] Default creds , admin/sipcapture` ## Steps to reproduce 1. Sign in to the HOMER web interface. 2. Open the widget named **PCAP Uploader**. (add via the top right `Widget` button if missing) 3. Select a valid previously saved PCAP file. 4. Start the upload. 5. Observe the error: ```text Invalid authorization header ``` 6. Open the browser Developer Tools and inspect the request headers to: ```text POST /api/v4/imports/pcap ``` 7. Observe that the request contains: ```http Authorization: undefined ``` ## Actual result The PCAP upload fails because the UI sends: ```http Authorization: undefined ``` ## Expected result The PCAP Uploader should retrieve the active authenticated session token and send an authorization header similar to: ```http Authorization: Bearer ``` The PCAP file should then be accepted and imported. ## Manual API validation I verified that the PCAP import endpoint itself works when a valid bearer token is supplied manually. First, I obtained a token using: ```http POST /api/v4/auth/sessions ``` I then uploaded the PCAP using: ```http POST /api/v4/imports/pcap Authorization: Bearer ``` The upload succeeded. This suggests that the backend PCAP import endpoint is operational and that the problem is likely in the PCAP Uploader widget's handling of the current authentication token. ## Additional diagnostics Failed browser request: ```http POST /api/v4/imports/pcap HTTP/1.1 Authorization: undefined ``` HTTP response status: ```text HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized ``` Response body: ```text { "error": { "type": "https://api.homer/errors/unauthorized", "title": "Unauthorized", "status": 401, "detail": "Invalid authorization header" } } ``` Relevant container logs: ```text homer | 2026-07-10T16:35:26+03:00 POST /api/v4/imports/pcap 401 20.905µs ``` ## Security note All passwords, bearer tokens, cookies, private addresses, and sensitive PCAP content have been removed from this report.

title: Regression in 11.0.288: Virtual field using data_extra_json + match=equals fails with JSON keys containing hyphens
author: Joonake
body:
I believe this is a regression introduced in **11.0.288**.

### Environment

- HOMER 11.0.288
- Fresh install from the official `.deb` package
- DuckDB bundled with 11.0.288

### Configuration

A SIP custom header is extracted successfully using:

```json
"custom_headers": [
  "X-Customer-ID"
]
```

The captured event contains:

```json
{
  "data_extra": {
    "custom_headers": {
      "X-Customer-ID": "12345"
    }
  }
}
```

Virtual field:

```json
{
  "id": "customer_id",
  "name": "Customer ID",
  "type": "string",
  "form_type": "input",
  "virtual": {
    "kind": "data_extra_json",
    "path": "custom_headers.X-Customer-ID",
    "match": "equals"
  }
}
```

### Generated SQL

When searching for value `12345`, HOMER generates the following SQL:

```sql
SELECT ...
WHERE data_extra ->> '$.custom_headers."X-Customer-ID"' = '12345'
```

### Error

DuckDB returns:

```
Conversion Error: Failed to cast value to numerical

LINE 1:
... AND data_extra ->> '$.custom_headers."X-Customer-ID"' = '12345'
```

The search returns HTTP 500.

### Expected behavior

The generated SQL should work correctly. For example, either of the following appears to be valid:

```sql
(data_extra ->> '$.custom_headers."X-Customer-ID"') = '12345'
```

or

```sql
json_extract_string(data_extra, '$.custom_headers."X-Customer-ID"') = '12345'
```

From the release notes it appears that support for hyphenated JSON keys was added previously, so the mapping itself seems to be correct. The generated SQL, however, fails on 11.0.288.

Could you please verify whether this is a regression introduced by the change from `json_extract()` to the `->>` operator?

This looks related to the previous fix for hyphenated JSON keys:

Issue #844: [https://github.com/sipcapture/homer/issues/844](https://github.com/sipcapture/homer/issues/844)
PR #847: [https://github.com/sipcapture/homer/pull/847](https://github.com/sipcapture/homer/pull/847)

According to the 11.0.284 release notes, custom_headers.X-Customer-ID should be supported. In 11.0.288 the mapping is accepted and the SQL is generated, but the generated query fails with a DuckDB conversion error. It looks like this may be a regression introduced by the switch from json_extract() to the ->> operator for match=equals

title: Equals search on data_extra-backed fields never returns matches
author: danieldonoghue
body:
**Version:** 11.0.287

## Description

Exact-match (`equals`) searches on fields backed by `data_extra` always return 0 results, even when matching records exist.

This affects:

* Virtual fields configured with:

  * `fields_mapping[].virtual`
  * `kind: data_extra_json`
  * `match: equals`
* Built-in `data_extra`-backed filters, including:

  * `from_host`
  * `to_host`
  * `user_agent`
  * `request_uri`
  * custom SIP headers

## Steps to reproduce

1. Store a value in `data_extra`, for example:

```json
{
  "path": "example-value"
}
```

2. Run an exact-match search for:

```
path = example-value
```

3. The query returns no rows, although records containing the value exist.

## Observed SQL

The generated clauses in `transactions_v4.go` use:

```sql
CAST(json_extract(data_extra, '$.path') AS VARCHAR) = 'example-value'
```

However, in DuckDB `json_extract()` returns the JSON representation of the value. For string values this includes quotes:

```sql
json_extract(data_extra, '$.path')
```

returns:

```
"example-value"
```

Therefore the comparison becomes effectively:

```sql
"example-value" = 'example-value'
```

which never evaluates to true.

## Expected behavior

The documented `match: equals` behavior should perform an exact match against the extracted string value.

`SEARCH_MAPPINGS_AND_FIELDS.md` describes `equals` as:

> exact match on extracted string

and provides examples such as `custom_headers.X-icc_uuid`, but these searches currently never match.

## Possible fix

Use a scalar JSON extraction function instead, for example:

```sql
json_extract_string(data_extra, '$.path') = 'example-value'
```

or:

```sql
data_extra ->> '$.path' = 'example-value'
```

Both return the unquoted string value and should make exact matching work as documented.

## Additional notes

This appears to affect all string comparisons where the source field is stored in `data_extra`, not only custom virtual fields.

title: Question: Is there a way for HOMER to notify when RTP packets are available for a call?
author: happytimor
body:
Hi,
I would like to monitor call quality, and I have configured sampled RTP packet capture in Kamailio.

Currently, I am facing two issues:
1. After a call is completed, RTP data may not always be available.
2. Even when RTP packets are captured, the data may be delayed.

Because of this, I would like HOMER to actively notify my system when RTP packets are available for a call.
Is there any existing feature in HOMER that supports this kind of notification or callback mechanism?
Thanks!

title: Question about REFER in the calls table
author: two56
body:
I was wondering if REFER requests are expected to show up in the calls table?

I have a feeling they used to appear there in Homer 7, and I found it helpful to see them as part of the call flow.

It's possible I’m just missing something or have a misconfiguration.

Any guidance would be much appreciated.

title: Brackets in From displayname not correctly parsed
author: btriller
body: From sipparser does not skip brackets in displayname. E.g.  `"a <b@c>" <sip:foo@bar>` results in user being `foo` but `b`. #845 extends tests to surface that edge case.

title: Custom-header search via data_extra_json virtual field fails — path parser rejects hyphen in key (custom_headers.X-icc_uuid)
author: yberladin
body:
# Question: how to make search by a custom SIP header (`X-icc_uuid`) work? (Config issue or expected behavior?)

> This is a **help / configuration question**, not a bug report. The custom header is captured and visible in `data_extra.custom_headers`, but searching by it in the UI returns all rows. I'd like to understand whether I've configured the mapping incorrectly, or whether additional setup (e.g. a heplify capture agent) is required.

## Environment

| | |
|---|---|
| **homer-core** | `11.0.283` |
| **OS** | Debian 12 |
| **Storage** | DuckLake / Parquet (DuckDB) |

## Goal

Search calls by a custom SIP header (`X-icc_uuid`) that is stored in `data_extra.custom_headers`.

## What I configured

**1.** Custom header extraction in `/usr/local/homer-core/etc/homer.json`:

```json
"sip": {
  "aleg_ids": ["X-call_uuid"],
  "custom_headers": ["X-icc_uuid"],
  "force_aleg_id": true
}
```

**2.** Restarted homer-core.

**3.** The header IS stored — this query works directly in DuckDB and returns the expected values:

```sql
 select data_extra from  homer.hep_proto_1_call where data_extra  LIKE '%X-icc_uuid%' limit 5;
```

**4.** Added a virtual search field to the call protocol mapping:

```json
  {
    "form_type": "input",
    "hide": false,
    "id": "x_icc_uuid",
    "index": "none",
    "name": "x_icc_uuid",
    "position": 21,
    "type": "string",
    "virtual": {
      "kind": "data_extra_json",
      "match": "equal",
      "path": "custom_headers.x_icc_uuid""
    }
  },
```

**5.** Searched in the UI using that field with a known value.

## What happens

homer-core logs:

```
WRN virtual search: fields_mapping virtual parse failed
error="field \"x_icc_uuid\": invalid path segment \"X-icc_uuid\" in \"custom_headers.X-icc_uuid\""
```

The Phase 1 `V4TransactionsSearch` query is then generated **with no WHERE filter**, so the search returns all rows:

```sql
SELECT * FROM homer_lake.main.hep_proto_1_call
WHERE timestamp >= ... AND timestamp <= ...
ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 50
```

For comparison, a `data_extra_json` field on a non-hyphenated key (`request_uri`) works and generates a proper filter:

```sql
AND CAST(json_extract(data_extra, '$.request_uri') AS VARCHAR) LIKE '%<value>%'
```

## Questions

1. **Is the mapping `path` syntax correct for a hyphenated key?** The parser rejects `custom_headers.X-icc_uuid` ("invalid path segment"), but the same path works fine in DuckDB. Is there a different/escaped syntax I should use for keys containing `-` (e.g. quoted or bracketed)?
2. **Is additional configuration required to make custom headers searchable** beyond `ingest.sip.custom_headers` + the mapping field? For example, do I need a **heplify capture agent**, or does homer-core's own ingest parser handle this end to end?
3. Is the **silent fallback** (failed virtual parse → no filter → all rows returned) expected, or should a failed mapping surface an error?

## Notes / what I've tried

- Searching the raw `payload` column (`payload LIKE '%<value>%'`) works as a workaround, but matches the value anywhere in the SIP message, not the specific header.
- I'm aware of #728 / #821 (custom_headers/aleg_ids not populated via indexed ENV vars) — but in my case extraction works (step 3), so that seems separate.

Any guidance on the correct configuration would be appreciated. Thanks!

title: Disable Games / NetGames
author: eimann
body:
Hi,

how can I disable Games and NetGames in the UI? These are not needed in a production environment and should be disabled.
I wasn't able to find config option documentation in docs/VOIPGames.md.

title: [homer11] --compaction-retention-days fails with "Binder Error: Cannot compare  values of type TIMESTAMP and type INTEGER_LITERAL" when run externally
author: lucascolaress
body:
Version: 11.0.278
DuckDB: v1.5.3

Steps to reproduce:
docker exec homer /homer system --compaction-retention-days 1

Error:
Retention failed: Binder Error: Cannot compare values of type TIMESTAMP 
and type INTEGER_LITERAL - an explicit cast is required
WHERE timestamp < 1782151963965931768

Note: Retention works correctly when run internally by the Homer service 
(CompactionService), but fails when triggered externally via CLI.

title: [homer11] Killer features homer 11 UI could have
author: gedia
body:
Hello,

I'm glad to see homer 11 has introduced several game-changing features, such as IP exclusion from pcap export.

To further build on that, I'd like to suggest a couple more which I believe would greatly improve usability. Up until homer 7, I'd often find myself exporting pcaps and opening them using sngrep for these reasons/features:

* The ability to navigate the call flow using the keyboard.
  * Selecting a SIP message using arrow keys
  * Showing a preview of the selected message
* The ability to compare SIP messages side-by-side in a diff

Here's my recommendation for a UI that would accomplish these, although I suspect it might be a tall order:
* When in call flow, (up/down) arrow keys can be used to highlight one message at a time. Highlight could be already placed at the message the user clicked on the search results table to bring up the call flow itself.
  * By highlight, I mean the entire row, pretty much like a mouse-over will highlight it now. Maybe a bit higher contrast?
* Pressing space does two things:
  * Selects the highlighted message
  * If this is the first message to be selected in the flow, then open a message preview, not in a floating window, but in a docked area taking up, say 1/3 of the call-flow view at the bottom. Ensure the visible part of the call-flow (now 2/3 of the window size) includes the selected message.
  * With one message selected, one is still able to navigate the flow using arrow keys. If the navigate to another msg and select it (again using space), then the preview area is split in the middle, showing:
    * The message one selected first on the left side
    * The message one selected second on the left side
    * Diff highlighting is applied to show any differences
    * The order is always determined by user selection, not any message timestamps
    * Block arrow key navigation in this state
* Pressing space again will de-select a message:
  * When deselecting in diff mode (vertically split preview), just close the pane which showed the preview of the now deselected message
  * When deselection in preview mode, close the preview panel altogether.
* Bringing up the preview pane should additionally be possible without selecting a message. Selection of a message is just a shortcut to make the preview appear, and deselection a shortcut to make it disappear. But additionally:
  * Introduce an icon or toggle for the preview pane, for example next to the "Filters" button.
  * When enabled, the preview pane is always visible
  * When enabled, navigating with the arrow keys will show the currently highlighted message, even without selecting it (using space)
  * Selecting a message in this state does nothing (from a UI perspective)
  * Selecting/Deselecting a second message in this state shows/hides the diff between the two selected messages, as before
  * Deselecting the only selected message in this state does nothing (from a UI perspective)
* Additional convenient option: another toggle which when enabled
  * will enforce "compact" view of messages whenever the preview pane is visible (regardless of how it was made visible)
  * will automatically respect the "compact" setting in the filters menu when the preview pane is hidden

Hope this makes sense to more people than just myself. Thank you for your ongoing work on this project!

title: [homer11] UI: Pressing ESC should close floating windows
author: gedia
body:
Hello,

This is a feature request similar to #499.

To re-iterate, if any floating windows have been opened (e.g. message view, or dialog/flow view), it would be convenient to be able to close them using key presses.

In homer 5 and 7, the ESC key served this purpose.

This feature is missing from homer 11, tested on 2026-06-19. Specifically, the UI should:

* save the order in which floating windows acquired focus
* upon pressing ESC, close the window in focus and bring in focus the window which most recently had it
* recurse this logic

Thanks!
George

title: [homer11] Introduce deduplication logic to take capture_id into account
author: gedia
body:
Hello,

I've been testing out homer 11 in docker. One feature I'd like to see in the future is deterministic deduplication in case the same message is received from two capture servers with differing capture_ids. For example, consider this flow:

Legend:
* 10.99.0.100:5060 -> **UAC** for the initial transaction
* 10.99.0.10:5060 -> **edge-proxy-ext** does RR
* 10.10.30.10:5060 -> **edge-proxy-int** does RR
* 10.10.20.10:5060 and 5062 -> **intermediate-proxy**
* 10.10.25.10:5062 -> **UAS** for the initial transaction

Now assuming both the **edge-proxy** (multihomed) and the **intermediate-proxy** (single-homed, not that it matters) both capture SIP and relay via HEP, we would get the following flow in homer with deduplication off:

```
   10.99.0.100:5060              10.99.0.10:5060               10.10.30.10:5060              10.10.20.10:5060              10.10.20.10:5062              10.10.25.10:5062 
──────────┬─────────          ──────────┬─────────          ──────────┬─────────          ──────────┬─────────          ──────────┬─────────          ──────────┬─────────
          │        INVITE (SDP)         │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │ ──────────────────────────> │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │  100 trying -- your call is │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │ <────────────────────────── │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │        INVITE (SDP)         │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │ ──────────────────────────> │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │        INVITE (SDP)         │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │ ────────────────────────>>> │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │  100 trying -- your call is │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │ <────────────────────────── │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │  100 trying -- your call is │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │ <<<──────────────────────── │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │                             │                             │        INVITE (SDP)         │         
          │                             │                             │                             │                             │ ──────────────────────────> │         
          │                             │                             │                             │                             │         100 Trying          │         
          │                             │                             │                             │                             │ <────────────────────────── │         
          │                             │                             │                             │                             │        200 OK (SDP)         │         
          │                             │                             │                             │                             │ <────────────────────────── │         
          │                             │                             │        200 OK (SDP)         │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │ <────────────────────────── │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │        200 OK (SDP)         │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │ <<<──────────────────────── │                             │                             │         
          │        200 OK (SDP)         │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │ <────────────────────────── │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │             ACK             │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │ ──────────────────────────> │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │                                           ACK                                           │         
          │                             │                             │ ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────> │         
          │             BYE             │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │ ──────────────────────────> │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │                                           BYE                                           │         
          │                             │                             │ ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────> │         
          │                             │                             │                                         200 OK                                          │         
          │                             │                             │ <────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │         
          │           200 OK            │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │ <────────────────────────── │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │                             │                             │                             │         
          │                             │                             │                             │                             │                             │         

```

As you can see, everything exchanged between `10.10.30.10:5060` and `10.10.20.10:5060` is duplicated. Not because there's retransmissions taking place, but because both hops perform SIP capture.

My proposal is for the following to be implemented with a new config option (say HEP_SETTINGS.DeduplicateDiffCID or similar), so that one can still capture retransmissions without getting duplicates due to dual/triple capture from HEP emitters:
> When receiving a message identical to one received before, but the current copy has a different capture_id, discard it without applying any other criteria. If enabled, perform this check before the already existing deduplication logic. Obviously, no further dedup checks are to be performed if the message is discarded as part of this "duplicate CID check".

Additionally, for the already implemented deduplication logic (based on retransmission timers), it would be convenient to make the T1 timer configurable (right now it's hard coded to 500e6 in decoder.go, i.e. 500 ms).

title: Homer 11 highly available architecture
author: nzapponi-w
body:
Hi, I am considering migrating from Homer 7 to 11, but I'm confused about the architecture of the catalog + storage.

It feels that the catalog sqlite is a single point of failure, and that the ingestion layer is not something that can be parallelized.
Is this correct? How do you recommend achieving a HA solution end-to-end?

Thanks

title: Homer 11: callee field not getting populated in most cases
author: flantel
body: Hi, Running the latest available 11.0.275 using docker-compose based on the examples/docker/docker-compose.yaml one. The hep client is Kamailio 5.8 using siptrace. I am running this version on multiple sites, and on only one of them in the homer UI the callee field is not populated. In any given call, it might be populated for the 100, ACK and 407, but not for the rest of the dialog. I have completely wiped and re-installed from scratch with the same result. Inspecting the INVITE and other requests show they all have both a To and RURI so I am at a loss as to why they are not being stored.

title: sqlite corrupted after update
author: fredrik-dahlgren
body:
After updating the image to `ghcr.io/sipcapture/homer@sha256:439a90ba4ad1597531396aea86a48dc5255761cd7ba55f3eee369125cae272a4` (`docker compose pull && docker compose up -d`) the application started and seemingly behaved as expected for a few minutes until queries started failing.

Logs:
```
homer-1  | 2026-06-15T06:15:08Z ERR Hub: Query failed on node local: query error: Invalid Input Error: Corrupt DuckLake - multiple snapshots returned from database
homer-1  | 2026-06-15T06:15:08Z WRN LPMappingSync: initial sync failed err="discover lp tables: node local: query error: Invalid Input Error: Corrupt DuckLake - multiple snapshots returned from database" 
```

There were multiple snapshots but deleting them failed on one table with `database disk image is malformed`

It was finally solved via
```
sqlite3 /data/homer/homer_catalog.sqlite ".dump" | sqlite3 /data/homer/homer_catalog_fixed.sqlite

mv /data/homer/homer_catalog.sqlite /data/homer/homer_catalog_corrupt.sqlite.bak
mv /data/homer/homer_catalog_fixed.sqlite /data/homer/homer_catalog.sqlite
```

```
DELETE FROM ducklake_snapshot WHERE snapshot_id > 232335;
DELETE FROM ducklake_snapshot_changes WHERE snapshot_id > 232335;
-- This originally failed with the malformed disk image warning before the DB salvage
DELETE FROM ducklake_data_file WHERE data_file_id >= 232138;
```

```
DELETE FROM ducklake_table 
WHERE table_name = 'hep_proto_1_siprec' 
AND rowid NOT IN (SELECT MIN(rowid) FROM ducklake_table WHERE table_name = 'hep_proto_1_siprec');

DELETE FROM ducklake_column 
WHERE table_id = 313 
AND rowid NOT IN (SELECT MIN(rowid) FROM ducklake_column WHERE table_id = 313 GROUP BY column_name);

DELETE FROM ducklake_schema_versions 
WHERE table_id = 313 
AND rowid NOT IN (SELECT MIN(rowid) FROM ducklake_schema_versions WHERE table_id = 313 GROUP BY schema_version);
```

Considering the issue i don't think the update itself was related, but possibly that docker spazed out and had two running containers. Or just general file corruption, i guess.

title: JWT session: HttpOnly cookie auth for multi-tab UI (replace tab-scoped sessionStorage)
author: adubovikov
body:
## Problem

Users reported that every new browser tab opened for Homer search deep links required a **new login**. This was especially painful when linking from external tools (Call-ID drill-down, shared search URLs).

Root cause: the bundled UI stored the session JWT in **`sessionStorage`** (`homer_v4_token`) after PR #756 (CodeQL security pass). `sessionStorage` is **tab-scoped**, so a new tab has no token even on the same Homer origin.

User feedback (paraphrased):
> Is keeping login sessions in session storage a design decision? We do a lot of linking to homer searches and each search = new tab = new login. Why not use a cookie instead of session?

## Security context

| Storage | Multi-tab | XSS exposure |
|---------|-----------|--------------|
| `sessionStorage` | No | Token readable from JS while tab is open |
| `localStorage` | Yes | Token readable from JS until expiry/logout |
| **HttpOnly cookie** | Yes | JWT not accessible to JavaScript |

`sessionStorage` was intentional (CodeQL #756), but the UX trade-off was unacceptable for multi-tab deep links.

## Solution (implemented)

### Backend
- Set **`homer_session`** HttpOnly cookie on `POST /auth/sessions` and `POST /auth/oauth2/token`
- JWT middleware accepts **Bearer → cookie → `?access_token=`** (CLI/API unchanged)
- `DELETE /api/v4/auth/sessions/current` — logout revokes session + clears cookie
- CSRF defense: `SameSite=Lax` + `Origin`/`Referer` check on cookie-authenticated mutating requests
- Config: `coordinator.jwt.cookie_enable` (default true), `cookie_name`, `cookie_same_site`, `cookie_secure`

### Frontend
- API calls use `credentials: include`
- Default: session via cookie only (shared across tabs)
- Optional **Remember me** → JWT in `localStorage` for Bearer/WebSocket fallback
- Logout calls server revoke endpoint

### Docs
- `docs/UI_COORDINATOR_AUTH_AND_TOKENS.md`
- `docs/SEARCH_URL.md`
- `docs/HEP_STREAM.md`

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] Login in one tab; open search deep link in a new tab → no re-login (same browser profile)
- [x] JWT not required in JavaScript for normal UI use (HttpOnly cookie)
- [x] CLI/scripts still work with `Authorization: Bearer`
- [x] Logout invalidates server session and clears cookie
- [x] Optional Remember me documented and available on login form

## Version

Target: `11.0.249` (homer11 branch)

title: Aleg correlation stopped working
author: fredrik-dahlgren
body:
I had correlation working 'out of the box' with the included call correlation script on May 26th but some time after that it stopped working.

The calls are both ingested in homer and searchable. Neither call correlates with the other one.
X-CID header is included just as before.

I increased the timeout values in HOMER_COORDINATOR_CORRELATION_* with no difference, but i also wasn't seeing any timeouts being logged before that. Only relevant log i see is 
`2026-06-11T06:51:07Z INF correlation: reloaded scripts count=1`

Currently on 11.0.241 but it was also present on 11.0.236

Not sure how Homer would handle a downgrade 11.0.241 -> 11.0.199 so i have not tried it.

title: Feature Request: Pretty Print event Payloads
author: marrold
body: Hi all, Thanks for the latest Homer 11, the installation and usage is really straightforward and I appreciate the effort that's gone into it! I was wondering if it would be possible to Pretty Print events with JSON payload? They're currently escaped which makes them hard to read / copy / paste I am sending them with hlog() from Kamailio Thanks again Matthew
